summary: Deliverable is incomplete and would not execute. Critical issues: (1) Workflow references LicenseChecker.Tests.ps1 and fixture files (package.json, requirements.txt, license-config.json, mock-licenses.json) that are not provided—act would fail immediately. (2) PowerShell scripts missing Set-StrictMode and $ErrorActionPreference='Stop' (best-practices violation). (3) run-act-tests.ps1 asserts on package names ('express', 'gpl-lib', 'requests') whose content depends on non-existent fixtures; assertions are fragile. (4) Workflow actions (checkout@v4) not pinned to SHA. (5) continue-on-error: true in compliance-check obscures intent. The PowerShell functions themselves (Get-DependenciesFromManifest, Test-LicenseCompliance) are reasonably structured with good naming and error handling, but context and fixture dependencies make this unshippable. Task requires red/green TDD with test file first—no test file shown per task scope, but workflow depends on it existing.
